required_skills: New market entry strategy and go-to-market planning for geographic or vertical expansion
Market research and feasibility analysis for untapped market opportunities
Operational launch management — standing up new city, region, or segment operations from zero
Localization strategy including regulatory landscape assessment, local partnerships, and cultural adaptation
Cross-functional coordination to align product, sales, marketing, and operations for expansion launches
Data-driven market prioritization using demographic, competitive, and demand-side signals
Stakeholder and government relations management in new geographies
Financial modeling for market-entry business cases including revenue projections and break-even analysis
preferred_skills: Prior experience launching a new market, city, or country for a marketplace, SaaS, or consumer startup
Familiarity with regulatory and compliance frameworks across multiple jurisdictions (India, SEA, MENA, EU)
Experience building and managing local on-the-ground teams or city-level operations
Understanding of supply-demand dynamics in marketplace businesses and cold-start problem solutions
Exposure to international entity setup, tax structures, and cross-border legal requirements
Knowledge of localization strategy — pricing adaptation, language, product customization for new markets
Network within target expansion geographies or verticals to accelerate initial traction
responsibilities: Conduct comprehensive market assessment and opportunity sizing for prospective expansion geographies or verticals using quantitative demand signals and competitive landscape analysis
Develop detailed market entry playbooks covering GTM strategy, local partnership plans, regulatory requirements, operational setup, and 90-day launch milestones
Own the end-to-end operational launch of new markets — coordinating across product, legal, finance, marketing, and customer success to hit launch timelines
Build financial models and investment cases for new market entry, including revenue ramp assumptions, customer acquisition cost projections, and break-even timelines
Identify, negotiate, and onboard strategic local partners, distributors, or supply-side stakeholders required to unlock initial traction in the new market
Design and execute localized go-to-market campaigns in partnership with the growth and marketing teams, adapting messaging, pricing, and channels for local relevance
Establish and track market-specific KPIs — launch velocity, early retention, supply-demand balance, and payback period — reporting progress to leadership in weekly reviews
Navigate regulatory and compliance requirements in new geographies, coordinating with legal and government relations teams to secure necessary licenses or approvals
Manage relationships with local government bodies, industry associations, and community stakeholders to build brand trust and accelerate market penetration
Conduct post-launch retrospectives on completed market expansions, extracting learnings and codifying best practices into a repeatable, scalable expansion playbook
